negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing canonical scheduler exact-target no-catch-up processing. The old scheduling crate/path is absent locally, and the live parent-state due-window logic intentionally includes any execution index whose target block is within TTL rather than only exact target blocks.
related_but_insufficient_code: ritual-scheduled-verification::due_index_bounds computes a TTL window: start + i*frequency <= block <= start + i*frequency + ttl.
ritual-scheduled-block-building notes it replaces the old SchedulerServiceState-specific helper.

rationale: The current canonical processing path uses modulo-based firing and submits only calls whose execution block is an exact target block; non-target blocks are skipped before transaction construction.
statement: Canonical scheduled-call processing must not implicitly burst-catch-up missed schedule windows: it may submit a scheduled transaction only when the candidate execution block is an exact scheduled target for that call, and it skips blocks that are not valid targets.
suggested_falsification_tests: Advance several missed schedule windows and assert canonical scheduler submits no burst catch-up, only exact target-block executions.
Compare parent-state due-window builder behavior against canonical exact-target expectations.
summary: Canonical no-catch-up scheduler processing is absent from the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Current port lacks the canonical scheduler service path and its exact-target modulo firing rule.
title: Canonical scheduler skips missed windows instead of catch-up bursts
